name: Cloudsmith
license: Proprietary (closed-source hosted SaaS; the official cloudsmith-cli client is open source)
cost: Free tiers: Core ($0/mo, 500 MB artifact data / 1 GB package delivery, hard limits, individuals) and a free Open Source plan (50 GB / 200 GB for public OSS repos with a valid license). Paid: Pro $149/mo (5 GB artifact data, 25 GB delivery); Ultra and Enterprise are custom-quoted. Usage-based on-demand overage at $1.50/GB for both artifact data and package delivery (billed in 1 GB blocks); uploads are free and only outgoing bytes count toward delivery. Every workspace gets a 14-day trial of premium features. (Earlier Team/Velocity tiers were superseded.)
language: Proprietary/undisclosed backend (cloud-native SaaS on AWS); official CLI and API client are Python
last_updated: 2026 (actively developed; €61.5M/$72M Series C closed April 2026; continuous changelog releases)
perf_notes: No published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks. Performance story is a global CDN/edge cache serving artifacts from the nearest location, plus just-in-time indexing of upstreams to keep proxied indexes in sync after first fetch; a real-time unindexed fallback is the slowest path.
scale_notes: Fully managed, multi-tenant, multi-region HA SaaS; customers do not run or scale nodes. Global edge caching, enterprise SLAs and SOC 2 compliance on Ultra/Enterprise. No self-hosted/on-prem deployment offered.
summary: A fully managed, cloud-native universal artifact management SaaS (30+ formats including Python/PyPI) positioned around software supply-chain security, policy-as-code, and global CDN delivery.
extras: Multi-format repositories: a single repo can hold Python, Docker/OCI, npm, Maven, NuGet, Helm, Debian, RPM, Cargo, Go, Conda, CRAN, Hugging Face and 30+ formats side by side
Enterprise Policy Manager (EPM): policy-as-code via Open Policy Agent / Rego with allow / flag / quarantine / block outcomes over artifact metadata and generated SBOMs
Dependency firewall + 'Block Until Scan' gate on cache-and-proxy upstreams
OIDC identity federation for CI (GitHub Actions, GitLab, CircleCI, Bitbucket, Jenkins) exchanging provider JWTs for short-lived (~90 min) Cloudsmith tokens for publish/pull
Entitlement tokens for scoped, revocable, per-repo download access (token-as-username Basic auth)
Package promotion workflows (copy/move immutable packages across build/test/staging/prod repos)
SAML SSO with Group Sync (IdP group -> team mapping) and SCIM; 2FA/SSO enforcement; service accounts
Global CDN delivery with intelligent edge caching
Official Terraform provider, VS Code extension, GitHub Action, CircleCI orb, and Datadog integration (metrics, events, audit logs, policy violations)
Migration/import tooling from other registries; Cloudsmith Navigator public package-intelligence explorer
Sigstore cosign support and custom signing keys; CycloneDX SBOM generation (strongest for container images) consumable by EPM policies
